title: Final-stop window entry
summary: Flag rows where a fresh slick is not yet a feasible final stint now but becomes feasible for a stop on the next lap.
rationale: The run explored many final-window entry variants; they were locally strong but sometimes more duplicate-heavy than option-richness features. The mechanism is plausible because teams often wait until a compound can carry them to the finish before stopping.
implementation_hint: Compare fresh SOFT, MEDIUM, and HARD feasibility using current estimated laps remaining versus estimated laps remaining after one more lap. Add per-compound newly-viable flags, any/count newly-viable flags, softest newly-viable slick, and nearest boundary margin.
expected_effect: Small to medium ROC AUC gain with medium confidence; most useful for late-race threshold ranking.
risk: Boundary features can overfit approximate compound-life constants; retain signed margins and avoid many redundant aliases.
